title: descurainia incisa var. leptophylla
intro: Descurainia incisa var. leptophylla, also known as small-fruited wallflower, is a plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to the Pacific coast of North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in herbal preparations to promote health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting immune function. Descurainia incisa var. leptophylla is often used in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
